channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: INS Hansa SBTF to get Upgrades : India Prepares Shore-Based Test Facility for future Twin-Engine Deck Fighter Trials
item.description: India’s Shore Based Test Facility (SBTF) at INS Hansa, Goa—recognized as the only facility of its kind in Asia and among just three globally—is undergoing a series of targeted upgrades to prepare for trials of the Twin Engine Deck Based Fighter (TEDBF). As of early 2026, multiple tenders have been issued to recalibrate and modernize the facility, signaling a transition from Naval LCA testing to the more demanding TEDBF prototype phase.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NewSpace Unveils Multi-Layered UGV Warfare Ecosystem with Vanguard, Airawat, Pioneer and Scout Platforms
item.description: NewSpace Research & Technologies Pvt Ltd has taken a significant step toward expanding India’s autonomous warfare capabilities by unveiling a new lineup of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs), signalling its formal entry into the land systems domain. Rather than focusing on a single platform, the company is developing a family of interoperable UGVs designed to operate as part of a layered, network-centric combat architecture.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA Mk2 Engine Race Heats Up as Rolls-Royce and Safran Pitch Future-Proof Modular Designs
item.description: India’s high-stakes fighter engine decision for the AMCA Mk2 is evolving beyond a conventional procurement into a long-term strategic architecture debate, with Rolls-Royce and Safran now positioning their proposals around modular, upgradeable propulsion ecosystems rather than static engine designs.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: No India didn’t deploy INS Dhruv to track Pakistani Missile Test as claimed by Pro-Pakistani Website Report
item.description: A claim by a pro-Pakistani defence news website based in Malaysia alleging that India deployed its advanced missile tracking vessel INS Dhruv in response to a Pakistani NOTAM has come under scrutiny due to the absence of verifiable evidence. The report suggested that India reacted to a large exclusion zone—spanning roughly 415 to 450 kilometers off Pakistan’s coastline near Karachi, Ormara, Gwadar, and Sonmiani—by positioning INS Dhruv in the Arabian Sea to monitor a suspected missile test conducted between April 14 and 15, 2026.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tejas Mk1 Shines at Tarang Shakti 2024 with Reported 4-0 Edge Over EA-18G Growler
item.description: India’s flagship indigenous fighter, the LCA Tejas Mk1, has emerged as a major talking point following Exercise Tarang Shakti 2024, with circulating reports suggesting that the aircraft achieved a notable edge in simulated engagements against the Royal Australian Air Force’s EA-18G Growler. Conducted in two phases, with the second phase held at Jodhpur Air Force Station from August 30 to September 14, the multinational exercise brought together some of the most capable air platforms in the world for complex Large Force Engagements (LFE).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: F-35 for India Not “Blocked”: Misreport Twists Lockheed Martin’s Statement on Government-to-Government Process
item.description: A claim by a pro-Pakistani defence website operating from Malaysia that “India has been blocked from acquiring the F-35” significantly misrepresents the actual position articulated by Lockheed Martin. The report appears to have selectively interpreted and amplified remarks originally provided to idrw.org, distorting a procedural clarification into a geopolitical denial narrative.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russian Media Claims Pakistan May Get Turkey’s KAAN Fighter by 2028, But Doubts Persist
item.description: A recent report by Russian state-linked outlet Sputnik has sparked debate by claiming that Pakistan could receive Turkey’s fifth-generation KAAN fighter as early as late 2028 or early 2029. The report frames the development as a strategic breakthrough for the Pakistan Air Force (PAF), suggesting Islamabad may soon join the exclusive club of nations operating stealth fighters. However, a closer technical and financial analysis indicates that these claims are highly optimistic and potentially shaped by geopolitical signaling rather than grounded program realities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: GE Aerospace signs contract with Indian Air Force to help establish an In-Country Depot for F404-IN20 Engines
item.description: GE Aerospace today announced a contract with the Indian Air Force (IAF) to establish an in-country depot facility for the F404 -IN20 engines that power IAF’s Light Combat Aircraft Tejas fleet. The facility will be set up by the IAF with technical inputs from GE Aerospace and is expected to help India’s indigenous defense sustainment effort. Once operational, the facility will eliminate the need to depend on the overseas repair centers, significantly improving turnaround times.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India May Leverage IMEC As Alternative Trade Route Amid Chokepoint Risks: Report
item.description: India is well-positioned to consider the India–Middle East–Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC), not merely as a “commercial connectivity initiative” but as “a potential national-security-cum-economic hedge”. The current phase of geopolitical instability offers a political window to press partner countries to expedite implementation of critical rail-link agreements, enhance customs-and-standards harmonisation, and codify shared rules for energy-and-digital-corridor governance, a report said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: In Call With Trump, PM Modi Stresses On Keeping Strait Of Hormuz ‘Open And Secure’
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Tuesday received a call from US President Donald Trump with both leaders discussing the current situation in West Asia as well as ways to further strengthen the bilateral partnership. “Received a call from my friend President Donald Trump. We reviewed the substantial progress achieved in our bilateral cooperation in various sectors. We are committed to further strengthening our Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership in all areas,” PM Modi posted on X after the phone call.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: B-21 Raider demonstrates aerial refueling
item.description: Northrop Grumman is accelerating American airpower with the B-21 Raider aircraft, advancing rapidly through its flight test campaign to deliver exceptional warfighting capability. With demonstrated aerial refueling, the B-21 proves it can go anywhere in the world and hold any target at risk to deter and defeat threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: FS Misri discusses bilateral ties with German political leaders and officials
item.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri met German political leaders and senior government officials on Tuesday and discussed deepening bilateral strategic partnership across key pillars of cooperation, including trade, defence, technology and innovation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Cyber Security Integral To National Security, Says Prez Murmu At RRU Convocation
item.description: President Droupadi Murmu on Tuesday said that cyber security, data protection and digital diplomacy have become as critical as conventional military strength, while addressing the fifth convocation ceremony of Rashtriya Raksha University (RRU) in Gujarat.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: For India To Decide: US Ambassador Gor On New Delhi’s Potential Role In Resolving West Asia Conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump is not being selective about who could help bring the ongoing West Asia conflict to a close and would welcome any country’s involvement, including India, in efforts to end the war, the country’s Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: National Security Is A Collective Duty Of Citizens: NSA Ajit Doval After Receiving Honorary Doctorate
item.description: National Security Advisor (NSA) Ajit Doval on Tuesday said that national security ultimately depends on the morale of the nation and its citizens’ awareness, stressing that it cannot be defined solely by military strength or technological capability but must be understood as a collective national responsibility.
item.dc:creator: admin
